lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨੑੀ ਮੰਦੈ ਪੈਰੁ ਨ ਰਖਿਓ ਕਰਿ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਧਰਮੁ ਕਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: onaee mandai pair na rakhio kar sukrit dharam kamaaeaa |
lines.english: They do not place their feet in sin, but do good deeds and live righteously in Dharma.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨੑੀ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਤੋੜੇ ਬੰਧਨਾ ਅੰਨੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਥੋੜਾ ਖਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: onaee duneea torre bandhanaa an paanee thorraa khaaeaa |
lines.english: They burn away the bonds of the world, and eat a simple diet of grain and water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਬਖਸੀਸੀ ਅਗਲਾ ਨਿਤ ਦੇਵਹਿ ਚੜਹਿ ਸਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: toon bakhaseesee agalaa nit deveh charreh savaaeaa |
lines.english: You are the Great Forgiver; You give continually, more and more each day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਪਾਂ ਲੋਆਂ ਮੰਡਲਾਂ ਖੰਡਾਂ ਵਰਭੰਡਾਂਹ ॥
lines.translit: deepaan loaan manddalaan khanddaan varabhanddaanh |
lines.english: islands, continents, worlds, solar systems, and universes;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਰਖਾਂ ਬਿਰਖਾਂ ਤੀਰਥਾਂ ਤਟਾਂ ਮੇਘਾਂ ਖੇਤਾਂਹ ॥
lines.translit: purakhaan birakhaan teerathaan tattaan meghaan khetaanh |
lines.english: Men, trees, sacred shrines of pilgrimage, banks of sacred rivers, clouds, fields,

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਿਆਈ ਵਡਾ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: vaddiaaee vaddaa paaeaa |7|
lines.english: By His greatness, the Great Lord is obtained. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਮਿਤਿ ਜਾਣੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਸਰਾਂ ਮੇਰਾਂ ਜੰਤਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: so mit jaanai naanakaa saraan meraan jantaah |
lines.english: oceans, mountains, and all beings - O Nanak, He alone knows their condition.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਡਜ ਜੇਰਜ ਉਤਭੁਜਾਂ ਖਾਣੀ ਸੇਤਜਾਂਹ ॥
lines.translit: anddaj jeraj utabhujaan khaanee setajaanh |
lines.english: the four sources of creation - born of eggs, born of the womb, born of the earth and born of sweat;

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੰਤ ਉਪਾਇ ਕੈ ਸੰਮਾਲੇ ਸਭਨਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: naanak jant upaae kai samaale sabhanaah |
lines.english: O Nanak, having created the living beings, He cherishes them all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਕਰਣਾ ਕੀਆ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਭਿ ਕਰਣੀ ਤਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: jin karatai karanaa keea chintaa bhi karanee taah |
lines.english: The Creator who created the creation, takes care of it as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਕਰਤਾ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਨਿ ਉਪਾਇਆ ਜਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: so karataa chintaa kare jin upaaeaa jag |
lines.english: He, the Creator who formed the world, cares for it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਜੋਹਾਰੀ ਸੁਅਸਤਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਦੀਬਾਣੁ ਅਭਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: tis johaaree suasat tis tis deebaan abhag |
lines.english: Unto Him I bow and offer my reverence; His Royal Court is eternal.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਿਆ ਟਿਕਾ ਕਿਆ ਤਗੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak sache naam bin kiaa ttikaa kiaa tag |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the True Name, of what use is the frontal mark of the Hindus, or their sacred thread? ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਨੇਕੀਆ ਚੰਗਿਆਈਆ ਲਖ ਪੁੰਨਾ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: lakh nekeea changiaaeea lakh punaa paravaan |
lines.english: Hundreds of thousands of virtues and good actions, and hundreds of thousands of blessed charities,

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਤਪ ਉਪਰਿ ਤੀਰਥਾਂ ਸਹਜ ਜੋਗ ਬੇਬਾਣ ॥
lines.translit: lakh tap upar teerathaan sehaj jog bebaan |
lines.english: hundreds of thousands of penances at sacred shrines, and the practice of Sehj Yoga in the wilderness,

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਸੁਰਤੀ ਲਖ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪੜੀਅਹਿ ਪਾਠ ਪੁਰਾਣ ॥
lines.translit: lakh suratee lakh giaan dhiaan parreeeh paatth puraan |
lines.english: hundreds of thousands of divine understandings, hundreds of thousands of divine wisdoms and meditations and readings of the Vedas and the Puraanas

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਸੂਰਤਣ ਸੰਗਰਾਮ ਰਣ ਮਹਿ ਛੁਟਹਿ ਪਰਾਣ ॥
lines.translit: lakh sooratan sangaraam ran meh chhutteh paraan |
lines.english: hundreds of thousands of courageous actions and giving up the breath of life on the field of battle,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਕਰਣਾ ਕੀਆ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: jin karatai karanaa keea likhiaa aavan jaan |
lines.english: - before the Creator who created the creation, and who ordained coming and going,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮਤੀ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਕਰਮੁ ਸਚਾ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak matee mithiaa karam sachaa neesaan |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, all these things are false. True is the Insignia of His Grace. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਏਕੁ ਤੂੰ ਜਿਨਿ ਸਚੋ ਸਚੁ ਵਰਤਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa saahib ek toon jin sacho sach varataaeaa |
lines.english: You alone are the True Lord. The Truth of Truths is pervading everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂੰ ਦੇਹਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਚੁ ਤਾ ਤਿਨੑੀ ਸਚੁ ਕਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis toon dehi tis milai sach taa tinaee sach kamaaeaa |
lines.english: He alone receives the Truth, unto whom You give it; then, he practices Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਿਨੑ ਕੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਸਚੁ ਵਸਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai sach paaeaa jina kai hiradai sach vasaaeaa |
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, Truth is found. In His Heart, Truth is abiding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਚਿ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਕਾਹੇ ਆਇਆ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: vich duneea kaahe aaeaa |8|
lines.english: Why have they even come into the world? ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖ ਸਚੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੑੀ ਮਨਮੁਖੀ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh sach na jaananaee manamukhee janam gavaaeaa |
lines.english: The fools do not know the Truth. The self-willed manmukhs waste their lives away in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਗਡੀ ਲਦੀਅਹਿ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਭਰੀਅਹਿ ਸਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: parr parr gaddee ladeeeh parr parr bhareeeh saath |
lines.english: You may read and read loads of books; you may read and study vast multitudes of books.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਬੇੜੀ ਪਾਈਐ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਗਡੀਅਹਿ ਖਾਤ ॥
lines.translit: parr parr berree paaeeai parr parr gaddeeeh khaat |
lines.english: You may read and read boat-loads of books; you may read and read and fill pits with them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜੀਅਹਿ ਜੇਤੇ ਬਰਸ ਬਰਸ ਪੜੀਅਹਿ ਜੇਤੇ ਮਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: parreeeh jete baras baras parreeeh jete maas |
lines.english: You may read them year after year; you may read them as many months are there are.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜੀਐ ਜੇਤੀ ਆਰਜਾ ਪੜੀਅਹਿ ਜੇਤੇ ਸਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: parreeai jetee aarajaa parreeeh jete saas |
lines.english: You may read them all your life; you may read them with every breath.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਲੇਖੈ ਇਕ ਗਲ ਹੋਰੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਝਖਣਾ ਝਾਖ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak lekhai ik gal hor haumai jhakhanaa jhaakh |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, only one thing is of any account: everything else is useless babbling and idle talk in ego. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਤੀਰਥ ਭਵਿਆ ॥ ਤੇਤੋ ਲਵਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: bahu teerath bhaviaa | teto laviaa |
lines.english: The more one wanders at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, the more one talks uselessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਿਖਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਪੜਿਆ ॥ ਤੇਤਾ ਕੜਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: likh likh parriaa | tetaa karriaa |
lines.english: The more one write and reads, the more one burns.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹੁ ਵੇ ਜੀਆ ਅਪਣਾ ਕੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: sahu ve jeea apanaa keea |
lines.english: O my soul, you must endure the consequences of your own actions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਨੁ ਨ ਖਾਇਆ ਸਾਦੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: an na khaaeaa saad gavaaeaa |
lines.english: One who does not eat the corn, misses out on the taste.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਭੇਖ ਕੀਆ ਦੇਹੀ ਦੁਖੁ ਦੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: bahu bhekh keea dehee dukh deea |
lines.english: The more one wears religious robes, the more pain he causes his body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਨਿ ਵਿਗੂਤਾ ॥ ਕਿਉ ਜਾਗੈ ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੂਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: mon vigootaa | kiau jaagai gur bin sootaa |
lines.english: Through silence, he is ruined. How can the sleeping one be awakened without the Guru?

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bahu dukh paaeaa doojaa bhaaeaa |
lines.english: One obtains great pain, in the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਨ ਪਹਿਰੈ ॥ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਕਹਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: basatr na pahirai | ahinis kaharai |
lines.english: One who does not wear any clothes, suffers night and day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਗ ਉਪੇਤਾਣਾ ॥ ਅਪਣਾ ਕੀਆ ਕਮਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: pag upetaanaa | apanaa keea kamaanaa |
lines.english: One who goes barefoot suffers by his own actions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਲੁ ਮਲੁ ਖਾਈ ਸਿਰਿ ਛਾਈ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: al mal khaaee sir chhaaee paaee |
lines.english: One who eats filth and throws ashes on his head

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: andh na jaanai fir pachhutaanee |
lines.english: - that blind man does not know the Lord; he regrets and repents in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਹੈ ਬੇਬਾਣੀ ਮੜੀ ਮਸਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: rahai bebaanee marree masaanee |
lines.english: One who lives in the wilderness, in cemetaries and cremation grounds

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਕਿਛੁ ਥਾਇ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: vin naavai kichh thaae na paaee |
lines.english: Without the Name, nothing is of any use.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖਿ ਅੰਧੈ ਪਤਿ ਗਵਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh andhai pat gavaaee |
lines.english: the blind fool loses his honor.
